exam_name: Adult-Gerontology Primary Care Nurse Practitioner, Board Certified (AGPCNP-BC)

testing_centers.notes: Computer-based testing at Prometric test centers; online proctoring available for select exams

id_requirements: Government-issued photo ID required. Name must match ANCC registration exactly. RN license number required at check-in.
exam_rules: Closed-book. No notes or reference materials permitted. On-screen calculator provided where applicable. 60-day waiting period required before retaking. MSN or APRN graduate degree required for advanced practice NP certifications.
renewal_options: Earn 75 CE credits (contact hours) every 5 years. CE credits must be relevant to the certification specialty. Submit renewal application via the ANCC certification portal. Maintain current, active RN license throughout certification period.

prerequisites.description: Graduate-level (MSN or higher) NP program with adult-gerontology primary care focus
Current, active RN license in the US or its territories

retake_policy.notes: Must wait 60 days before retaking. Full exam fee required for each retake attempt. Check ANCC website for current retake policies.

official_study_resources.title: ANCC Certification Exam Test Content Outlines
official_study_resources.notes: Free downloadable test content outlines available for each specialty on the certification page

official_study_resources.title: ANCC Practice Test
official_study_resources.notes: Official practice tests available for purchase through the ANCC store

aliases: adult-gerontology primary care nurse practitioner board certified agpcnp-bc
